labels.analysis: The MCP Server "WaveGuard" provides 19 tools focused on data analysis & processing tasks. Core capabilities include analysis and computation, data access and reading, search and retrieval. Key tools: waveguard_scan, waveguard_scan_timeseries, waveguard_health, waveguard_fingerprint, waveguard_compare.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Data Analysis & Processing" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: data, metric, report, statistics.

tools.name: waveguard_scan
tools.description:
Find outliers and anomalies in structured data — ideal as a second step after pulling records from Google Sheets, Airtable, Supabase, Notion databases, HubSpot, Financial APIs, GitHub, NPM, or any source that returns rows of JSON. Fully stateless: send known-good rows as training and suspect rows as test in ONE call. Returns per-row anomaly scores, confidence levels, and the top features explaining WHY each row was flagged.

Typical workflow: (1) Pull data from another tool (e.g. Google Sheets, Supabase query, HubSpot deals). (2) Pass the first N rows as training (normal baseline). (3) Pass remaining or new rows as test. (4) Report which rows are anomalous and why.

Works on JSON objects, numbers, text, arrays. No separate training step required.

Examples:
- Spreadsheet QA: Pull 500 sales rows from Sheets → train on first 400 → test last 100 → flag outlier entries
- Financial screening: Get ratios for 50 stocks from a financial API → find anomalous ones
- CRM hygiene: Pull HubSpot deals → flag deals with unusual discount/value patterns
- Dependency audit: Get NPM package metrics → flag packages with anomalous quality scores
- Commit review: Pull GitHub commit metadata → flag unusual commit patterns

training.description: 2+ examples of NORMAL/expected data — the known-good baseline. Typically the bulk of rows from a spreadsheet, database query, or API response. All samples should be the same type/shape. More samples = better baseline (10-100 is ideal for tabular data).

test.description: 1+ data points to check for anomalies — new entries, recent rows, or the subset you want validated. Same type/shape as training. Each sample is scored independently.

sensitivity.description: Anomaly threshold multiplier (default: 2.0). Lower = more sensitive. Higher = less sensitive. Range: 0.5 to 5.0.

encoder_type.description: Data encoder type. Omit to auto-detect from data shape.

field_level.description: Physics field complexity. 0 = real scalar (default). 1 = complex field (phase-aware, 62-dim fingerprint).

tools.name: waveguard_scan_timeseries
tools.description:
Detect anomalies in time-series data — use after pulling numeric metrics from monitoring APIs, financial data sources, IoT sensors, or spreadsheet columns. Send a single numeric array and specify a window size. Early windows define 'normal', recent windows are tested for anomalies.

Typical workflow: (1) Pull a column of numbers from Sheets, a Supabase time-series table, or a metrics API. (2) Pass the array here. (3) Get back which time windows are anomalous.

Examples:
- Revenue monitoring: Pull monthly revenue from Sheets → detect anomalous months
- Stock screening: Pull 90 days of closing prices → find unusual price windows
- Server health: Pull response-time metrics → identify degradation windows
- Sensor QA: Pull temperature readings from IoT API → flag sensor drift

data.description: Numeric time-series array, ordered chronologically. Should have at least 3x window_size data points.

window_size.description: Number of data points per window (default: 10). Smaller windows detect finer-grained anomalies.

test_windows.description: Number of most recent windows to test (default: half of total windows). The rest are used as training (normal baseline).

tools.name: waveguard_health
tools.description: Check WaveGuard API health, GPU availability, version, and engine status. No authentication required. Returns status, version, and GPU info.

verbose.description: Return detailed health info including memory and uptime (default: false).

tools.name: waveguard_fingerprint
tools.description:
Get a physics embedding of any data item (52-dim at Level 0, 62-dim at Level 1 with phase statistics). The fingerprint captures structural properties via wave-equation dynamics — useful for similarity search, clustering, baseline comparison, and drift detection. Works on JSON objects, token metrics, wallet activity, trading data, or any structured data.

Returns a deterministic vector with labeled dimensions (chi statistics, energy distribution, gradient patterns, and phase coherence at Level 1).

data.description: Any data item to fingerprint: JSON object, numeric array, string, or structured record.

encoder_type.description: Data encoder. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_compare
tools.description:
Compare two data items for structural similarity using physics-based fingerprints. Returns cosine similarity (0–1) and Euclidean distance. Use for duplicate detection, behavioral matching, drift analysis, or checking if two tokens/wallets/contracts are structurally similar.

Cosine similarity > 0.95 = very similar. < 0.80 = structurally different.

data_b.description: Second data item to compare (same type as data_a).

encoder_type.description: Data encoder. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_token_risk
tools.description:
Assess crypto token legitimacy risk. Send metrics from known-good tokens as training (price, volume, holders, liquidity, market_cap, age_days, etc.) and suspect tokens as test. Detects pump-and-dump patterns, fake metrics, and anomalous token profiles.

Example: Pull CoinGecko data for 20 established tokens → train. Test a new token → get risk score and which metrics are suspicious.

training.description: 3+ known-good token metric objects. Each should include fields like price, volume_24h, market_cap, holders, liquidity, age_days, etc.

test.description: 1+ suspect token metric objects to evaluate.

tools.name: waveguard_wallet_profile
tools.description:
Profile wallet behavior against baselines. Send normal wallet transaction patterns as training (tx_count, avg_value, unique_tokens, gas_spent, active_days, etc.) and suspect wallets as test. Detects bot activity, wash trading wallets, and sybil patterns.

Example: Profile 50 organic wallets → test 10 suspect addresses.

training.description: 3+ known-organic wallet activity profiles.

tools.name: waveguard_volume_check
tools.description:
Detect wash trading and fake volume in OHLCV candle data. Send known-legitimate candles as training and suspect candles as test. Detects artificial volume spikes, suspiciously regular patterns, and manipulated price-volume relationships.

Example: Send 100 candles from a liquid pair as baseline, test candles from a suspicious pair.

training.description: 3+ OHLCV candle objects from known-legitimate trading. Fields: open, high, low, close, volume.

tools.name: waveguard_price_manipulation
tools.description:
Detect price manipulation in time-series data. Send a price or price+volume history as a numeric array. Early windows define 'normal' trading, recent windows are tested for manipulation patterns (pump-and-dump, spoofing, layering).

Example: Send 90 days of closing prices → detect manipulated windows.

data.description: Price time-series array (chronological). At least 20 data points.

test_windows.description: Number of recent windows to test (default: half).

tools.name: waveguard_market_data
tools.description:
Fetch live crypto market data from CoinGecko and DexScreener. No external data needed — WaveGuard pulls it for you.

Use 'coin_id' for CoinGecko (e.g. 'bitcoin', 'ethereum', 'solana').
Use 'contract_address' for DexScreener (any chain).
Use 'search' to find token IDs by name/symbol.

Returns: price, volume, market cap, liquidity, price history, OHLC candles — ready to feed into waveguard_token_risk, waveguard_volume_check, or waveguard_price_manipulation.

action.description:
What data to fetch:
- token_data: full metrics for a CoinGecko coin
- price_history: daily prices (for price_manipulation)
- ohlc: OHLC candles (for volume_check)
- top_coins: top N by market cap (training baseline)
- search: find CoinGecko coin IDs
- dex_token: DEX data by contract address
- dex_search: search DEX pairs

coin_id.description: CoinGecko coin ID (e.g. 'bitcoin', 'ethereum'). Required for token_data, price_history, ohlc.

contract_address.description: Token contract address (any chain). Required for dex_token.

query.description: Search query. Required for search, dex_search.

days.description: Number of days of history (default: 90 for price_history, 30 for ohlc).

tools.name: waveguard_counterfactual
tools.description: Run baseline plus counterfactual variants and measure verdict/score sensitivity.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples used to build the reference profile.

base_test.description: Baseline candidate sample to evaluate before counterfactual perturbations.

counterfactual_tests.description: 1+ perturbed variants of base_test for sensitivity analysis.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect from input structure.

sensitivity.description: Anomaly sensitivity multiplier (default: 1.0). Higher values flag more aggressively.

field_level.description: 0 = real scalar field (faster), 1 = complex field (richer phase dynamics).

tools.name: waveguard_trajectory_scan
tools.description: Analyze sequence drift and regime shifts over ordered samples.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples used to establish the reference regime.

sequence.description: Ordered samples (time sequence) to scan for drift and regime shifts.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_instability
tools.description: Estimate instability under controlled perturb-and-resolve trials.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples for reference dynamics.

test.description: 1+ candidate samples to stress-test with perturbation trials.

trials.description: Number of perturbation trials per sample.

perturbation_strength.description: Relative perturbation amplitude applied during instability assay.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_phase_coherence
tools.description: Measure coherence/entropy and collapse-risk indicators for candidate data.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples for reference coherence metrics.

test.description: 1+ candidate samples to evaluate for phase coherence and entropy.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

field_level.description: Field representation level. Default 1 for phase-aware analysis.

tools.name: waveguard_interaction_matrix
tools.description: Compute pairwise interaction matrix and cluster decomposition for entities.

training_context.description: 2+ baseline context samples used for normalization.

entities.description: 2+ entities to evaluate for pairwise interaction effects.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

field_level.description: Field representation level. Default 1 for interaction/phase features.

tools.name: waveguard_cascade_risk
tools.description: Estimate shock propagation and resilience from adjacency-linked entities.

training_context.description: 2+ baseline context samples used for normalization.

entities.description: 2+ entities/nodes participating in the cascade graph.

adjacency_matrix.description: N×N weighted adjacency matrix describing link strengths between entities.

shock_indices.description: Indices of initially shocked entities within the entities array.

shock_strength.description: Initial perturbation magnitude injected at shock indices.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

field_level.description: Field representation level. Default 1 for graph interaction dynamics.

tools.name: waveguard_mechanism_probe
tools.description: Run targeted interventions and rank effect sizes.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples used to construct the reference profile.

intervention_tests.description: 1+ intervention variants used to estimate effect sizes.

intervention_labels.description: Optional labels for intervention variants (same order as intervention_tests).

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_action_surface
tools.description: Score candidate actions and extract robust action zones.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples used to define the reference profile.

action_tests.description: 1+ candidate actions/scenarios to score against baseline.

action_labels.description: Optional labels for each action variant.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.

tools.name: waveguard_multi_horizon_outlook
tools.description: Compute horizon-specific anomaly outlook and consistency across windows.

training.description: 2+ baseline normal samples used to establish reference behavior.

sequence.description: Ordered sample sequence used for multi-horizon outlook analysis.

horizons.description: List of horizon lengths (in sequence steps) to evaluate.

encoder_type.description: Optional encoder override. Omit to auto-detect.
